Document Intelligence Studio
Choose what Midnight is creating, then move into a purpose-built authoring workspace tuned for that document type.
Step 1 · Document Type
Choose the authoring lane
Midnight changes the intake, structure, export path, and AI assistance based on what you are building. Start by selecting the document type, then work inside a dedicated workspace built for that outcome.
No type selected
Workspace
Select a document type
Each document type has its own authoring experience, media handling, and export profile.
Studio idle
Policy mode keeps the current Midnight policy pipeline intact: framework-aware preview, section editing, and .docx generation.
Policy Details
Policy Content
Compliance Frameworks
Generating...
Generating
Extracting requirements...
Edit Before Download
Ready to Edit
Review and edit each section. Changes apply to the generated .docx — nothing is saved until you click Download.
Procedure / SOP mode is now a real builder workspace. Steps can be added, reordered, assigned, and enriched with screenshots, clips, branching, and SLAs. The AI analysis hooks are staged in the UX, but the current repo does not yet expose a dedicated screenshot/video intelligence endpoint for this lane.
Build step-by-step operating procedures with role ownership, decision branches, timer expectations, and media placeholders that can later flow into Word, PowerPoint, HTML, PDF, and QR-ready outputs.
0 steps
SOP Export Studio
These export modes are now visible in the authoring lane so the SOP shape is clear before generation.
Word (.docx) with embedded images PowerPoint one slide per step Interactive HTML page PDF with QR code poster
Technical Standard
Use this for control requirements, architecture rules, and evidence-linked technical expectations.
Requirement statements with control mappings
Evidence links, implementation notes, and versioned baselines
Authoring Notes
Process Flow Storyboard
This lane is designed around swimlanes, transitions, checkpoints, and presentation exports.
Swimlane diagrams PowerPoint export Decision nodes Executive storyboard
Flow Inputs
Training Module
Build slide-based enablement with structured learning points, checkpoints, and downstream quiz generation.
One module, multiple slides, knowledge checks
Presentation-first narrative with replayable media
Module Shape
Slides Quiz prompts Completion tracking Speaker notes
Incident Runbook
Trigger-driven response playbooks with timers, escalation lanes, and decision thresholds.
Response triggers and activation criteria
Escalation paths, owners, and timing checkpoints
Runbook Inputs
Risk Assessment
This lane centers on a live risk register, likelihood/impact scoring, and control-gap mapping.
Risk register Impact matrix Control gaps Mitigation owners
Assessment Context
Audit Package
Curate evidence requests, findings logs, and framework checklists into one exportable audit bundle.
Evidence index and request tracker
Framework checklist and findings narrative
Package Contents
Evidence checklist Findings log ZIP export Control mapping
AI Governance Intake
Capture the AI system context once, then let Midnight stage the right governance artifacts, risk posture, and regulatory mapping around it.
Governance Outputs
This lane produces three linked artifacts from the same intake instead of a single flat policy.
AI Governance Policy
Internal policy covering permitted use, ownership, oversight, and accountability for the AI system.
Model Risk Assessment
Risk-focused view with control gaps, impact framing, and recommended mitigation structure.
Regulatory Mapping Summary
Crosswalk into current AI frameworks and healthcare/privacy intersections where relevant.
Auto-suggested posture
Moderate
Midnight will refine this classification based on the system type, output sensitivity, healthcare overlap, and whether the model influences decisions or generates customer-facing content.
Regulatory Mapping Targets
NIST AI RMFProfiles the govern, map, measure, and manage controls that should anchor this AI system.
EU AI ActStages a likely risk category based on whether the system assists, influences, or directly drives decisions.
ISO 42001Frames the management-system controls, ownership, and evidence expectations around the AI program.
Executive Order on AIHighlights governance areas relevant to trustworthy deployment, documentation, and accountability.
HIPAA + AIHealthcare-sensitive workflows trigger additional PHI handling and human-review expectations.
Snapshot
Select AI Governance and complete the intake to see a live summary of the system profile, risk posture, and likely governance outputs.
Step 4 · Media Embedding
Shared media studio
Every document lane can pull in screenshots, clips, and generated diagrams. This shared studio keeps those assets in one place while preserving tenant-aware authoring boundaries.
No document selected
Insert Screenshot
Upload PNG or JPG assets. The builder captures the file, stages a caption field, and leaves room for AI-generated descriptions once the media service is connected.
Word/PDF outputs can embed the captured image with a draft or edited caption.
Insert Video Clip
Upload MP4 or WebM clips. Midnight stages transcript/caption notes now and can later promote them into HTML playback and document snapshots.
HTML keeps playable clips. Word/PDF can later use a frame extraction path.
Insert Diagram
Describe the process in plain English and stage a swimlane or flowchart asset directly inside the authoring flow.
Embedded Assets
Assets added here can follow the selected document type into the final export path.
Step 5 · Export Studio
Destination-aware output paths
Select a document type to see how Midnight can package and deliver the final artifact.

Profile & Settings
Manage your owner profile, workspace defaults, and the values Midnight uses to prefill new runs.
MW
Owner Account
Workspace Owner
This workspace is running on a single-owner session. Your saved defaults flow into Create, Migrate, GRC Summary, and the dashboard shell.
Owner Environment
Email
admin@midnight.ai
Workspace ID
personal
Organization
Midnight Workspace
Session Status
Authenticated
What saves here: display name, workspace label, default industry, default policy owner, and your default GRC organization. Midnight uses those values to keep the tool feeling like your workspace instead of a blank form every time.
Workspace Defaults
These preferences are browser-saved for now, so they are fast and safe to adjust without adding a fake user system behind the scenes.
